prompt:
Export/import for the accounting screens keeps mangling anything with a comma or a quote in it, so I'm done with the string.split approach. Build csv.ts properly:

stringify(rows, opts?) — rows is an array of arrays of cells. Cells that contain the delimiter, a double quote, or a newline get wrapped in double quotes with inner quotes doubled (RFC 4180 style). Clean cells stay bare — I diff these files in review and don't want quote noise. Non-string cells are stringified with String(). Rows join with '\n'. opts.delimiter defaults to ','.

parse(text, opts?) — inverse of the above: handles quoted fields containing delimiters, doubled quotes, and embedded newlines (real ones, inside a quoted cell). Accepts \r\n and \n line endings. A trailing final newline is not an extra row; empty input is zero rows; a blank line in the middle IS a row with one empty field. 'a,' ends with an empty field. Malformed input must throw SyntaxError — that means an unterminated quoted field, or garbage between a closing quote and the next delimiter/end of line. opts.delimiter as above. opts.header: true makes parse return objects keyed by the first row instead of arrays.

Golden rule: parse(stringify(rows)) round-trips any all-string matrix bit-for-bit — the tests feed it fields full of commas, quotes, and newlines.

node --test test_csv.ts must pass without editing it. No dependencies.
